_comment: Shared base for trigger templates. Trigger JSONs can set "extends": "trigger_base" and will be deep-merged with this object (template wins).

create_trigger.description: Create a new trigger using this template

app_id.description: UUID of your Piwik PRO app

trigger_type.description: Must match this template (e.g. click, page_view, event)

conditions.description: Array of condition objects that determine when the trigger fires

relationships.description: Can include tags to associate with this trigger

name.description: Human-readable trigger name that describes its purpose
name.recommendation: Use descriptive names that clearly indicate what is being tracked

trigger_type.edit_note: ⚠️ Cannot be changed after trigger creation

conditions.description: Array of condition objects that define when the trigger should fire

condition_id.description: Unique identifier for this condition (auto-generated during trigger creation)

variable_id.description: UUID of the variable to check

created_at.description: When the trigger was created (ISO 8601 timestamp)
created_at.edit_note: 🚫 Auto-generated by API, cannot be modified

updated_at.description: When the trigger was last updated (ISO 8601 timestamp)
updated_at.edit_note: 🚫 Auto-generated by API, cannot be modified

is_published.description: Whether the trigger is published in the current version
is_published.edit_note: 🚫 Controlled by version publishing, cannot be directly modified

field_mutability_guide.description: Understanding which trigger fields can be edited after creation

editable.description: ✅ Can be updated anytime after trigger creation
editable.note: These fields can be safely modified through update_trigger

create-only.description: ⚠️ Can only be set during trigger creation, immutable afterward
create-only.edit_behavior: These fields will be ignored in update operations
create-only.note: Trigger type defines the fundamental behavior and cannot be changed

read-only.edit_behavior: These fields will be filtered out of update requests
read-only.note: Timestamps and publishing status are managed by the system

update_best_practices: Only include editable fields in update_trigger attributes
Create-only and read-only fields are automatically filtered out
Check field mutability before attempting updates
Use templates_get_trigger() to see all available fields and their mutability
Test trigger conditions after updates to ensure they work as expected

getting_variable_ids.step_1: Use variables_list() to see all available variables in your app
getting_variable_ids.step_2: Find the variable you need for your trigger conditions
getting_variable_ids.step_3: Copy the 'id' field from the variable object
getting_variable_ids.step_4: Use that UUID as the variable_id in your trigger conditions

step_1.purpose: Find the variable IDs for your conditions

step_2.purpose: Create the trigger with appropriate conditions

step_3.purpose: Associate the trigger with a tag

step_4.purpose: Verify trigger works correctly before going live
